classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "For what we look to is the conscience of the giver, to cleanse that of the recipient."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: We therefore need have no anxiety about the conscience of Christ.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But if you assert any man to be the giver, be he who he may, there will be no certainty about the cleansing of the recipient, because there is no certainty about the conscience of the giver.
